groups.pid_desc: PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) controller for precise wheel positioning and feedback response.

settings.min_log_level_desc: Set the minimum detail level for backend server logs.
settings.debug_desc: Show raw hardware data and logs in Monitor tab

help.motion_range: Range of motion where device reports value change on joystick axis. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Corresponds to ENCODER range! If you have reductions in your device, take it into account.
help.total_effect_strength: Global Strength: Defines the maximum output power of all effects.
help.static_dampening_strength: Adds dampening force in normal range of motion. Static dampening determines control movement and tries to resist it. Always constant.
help.dynamic_dampening_strength: Additional dampening force in normal range. Not constant and depends on complex rules based on overall force produced. Works with FFBeast Commander calculations. ⚠️ Has no effect if FFBeast Commander is not running.
help.integrated_spring_strength: From v24.1.4, there's possibility to have spring always working. Controls strength of such integrated spring that returns control to center (useful for games without FFB).
help.power_limit: Controls overall device power. Depends on PSU power and motor heating. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Tune this value specifically for your motor and PSU combination! Increase by small increments (5-10 clicks) and check if PSU holds load and motor doesn't overheat.
help.braking_limit: When motor spins quickly, it works as a generator raising bus voltage. Dissipating excess voltage on braking resistor prevents PSU shutdown. Start with low values (5-10) and increase gradually if PSU turns off during quick rotations.
help.pole_pairs: Number of motor pole pairs (number of magnets ÷ 2). Hoverboard motors usually have 15 pole pairs. Check your motor specifications.
help.amplifier_gain: Sensitivity adjustment for the motor driver.
help.proportional_gain: Proportional (P): Defines how fast the motor reacts to position error.
help.integral_gain: Integral gain to limit steady-state error.
help.direct_x_constant_strength: Scales the strength of Constant effects from games.
help.direct_x_periodic_strength: Scales the strength of Periodic effects (Sine, Square, Triangle).
help.direct_x_spring_strength: Scales the strength of Spring effects requested by the game.
help.calibration_speed: Movement speed during calibration. If control moves too quickly during calibration, decrease to adequate values.
help.calibration_magnitude: Power with which starting calibration sequence performs. Set as low as possible where calibration still works. Starting values: wheel=5, flight controls=10. If device has movement resistance, increase gradually.
help.encoder_direction: Inverts the motor encoder reading. Use if the position increases to the wrong side.
help.force_direction: Inverts the output force direction. Use if the wheel pushes away instead of centering.
help.debug_torque: Sends raw torque values to logs for debugging.
help.invert_game_force: Inverts the direction of Constant Force effects coming from the game.
help.force_enabled: Enables/disables force generation on motors. When unchecked, the device will not produce forces. ⚠️ REQUIRES REBOOT to take effect.
help.edit_mapping: Configure button and keyboard mappings for this axis
help.axis_invert: Inverts the direction of the analog signal reading.
help.soft_stop_strength: Strength of the soft stop barrier effect.
help.soft_stop_range: Range (degrees/units) where soft stop gradually engages.
help.soft_stop_dampening: Dampening force applied when hitting the soft stop.
help.encoder_cpr: Counts Per Revolution (CPR) of the encoder.
help.speed_buffer: Size of the buffer used to smooth speed calculation.
help.axis_custom_name: Friendly name for identifying this axis in the UI and game mappings.
help.axis_buttons_low: Defines the percentage threshold where the virtual button associated with the low axis value will be activated.
help.axis_buttons_high: Defines the percentage threshold where the virtual button associated with the high axis value will be activated.
help.spi_latch_delay: Wait time after latching before starting SPI transfer. Helps synchronization on slower shift registers.
help.spi_pulse: Length of the clock pulse (Clock speed). Increase this if you experience noise or unreliable data on long cables.
help.axis_min: The minimum raw value expected from the sensor. Values below this will be clamped to 0%.
help.axis_max: The maximum raw value expected from the sensor. Values above this will be clamped to 100%.

messages.keyboard_warning: Note: Keyboard mapping requires the 'Mapping Service' to be running.
messages.mappings_loaded: Loaded {count} axis keyboard mappings from config

tools.ffb_desc: Live manual Force Feedback testing. Sliders reset to 0 when released.
tools.mapping_desc: Maps buttons and axes to keyboard keys. Enable this to use the wheel in games without native support.

desc.title: Description
desc.none: No extension protocol active. The wheel operates in standalone mode without external peripherals.
desc.buttons: Extends the available buttons using GPIO pins. Adds up to 32 digital buttons through direct pin mapping.
desc.tm: Thrustmaster SPI protocol for connecting original TM shifters and button boxes. Uses SPI communication for fast, reliable button states.
desc.fanatec: Fanatec SPI protocol for connecting original Fanatec shifters and handbrakes. Compatible with CSL and ClubSport accessories.
desc.custom: Custom SPI mode. Allows manually defining all SPI communication parameters for natively unsupported devices.
desc.tm_style: Configuration for 3x CD4021 shift registers in series. Used in Thrustmaster wheel extensions and similar DIY projects.
desc.style_165: Support for 3x SN74HC165 expansion boards, common in modules bought from Aliexpress.
desc.vpc: Native support for Virpil (VPC) grips and extensions.

compat.title: Compatibility
compat.buttons: Any momentary switch or button connected directly to GPIO pins. Supports both common ground and common VCC configurations.
compat.tm: Thrustmaster TH8A shifter, sequential shifter, and official button boxes. Some third-party TM-compatible devices may also work.
compat.fanatec: Fanatec CSL shifter, ClubSport shifter, and handbrake. Requires proper connector wiring (check pinout diagram).
compat.custom: Any generic SPI device. Requires technical knowledge of communication parameters.
compat.tm_style: Thrustmaster bases, original Thrustmaster rims or DIY boards based on CD4021 register.
compat.style_165: Blue/green SN74HC165 boards from Aliexpress. Connect to the 'IN' side of the board!
compat.vpc: Virpil rims and bases. Requires stable 5V power supply.

config.title: Configuration
config.buttons: Configure pin modes in the Pins tab. Set each GPIO pin to 'Button' mode and assign button numbers in the Buttons tab.
config.tm: Set SPI pins (CS, SCK, MISO) in the Pins tab. No additional calibration needed - the protocol auto-detects button presses.
config.fanatec: Set SPI pins (CS, SCK, MISO) in the Pins tab. Ensure correct power supply (usually 5V). Check connector pinout before connecting.
config.custom: Configure SPI pins in Pins tab and adjust Clock, Latch and SPI Mode parameters in this tab.
config.tm_style: Pin 3: SCK, Pin 4: MISO, Pin 5: nCS. Connect VCC to 3.3V.
config.style_165: Pin 3: SCK, Pin 4: MISO, Pin 5: nCS. Preferably power with 3.3V even if board says 5V.
config.vpc: Pin 3: SCK, Pin 4: MISO, Pin 5: nCS. VPC requires 5V to function!

tips.title: Tips & Troubleshooting
tips.none: To add buttons or shifters, select an appropriate extension mode above.
tips.buttons: Use pull-up or pull-down resistors for clean signals. Test each button in the Monitor tab after configuration. Debouncing is handled in firmware.
tips.tm: If buttons don't respond, check SPI wiring (especially ground). Some shifters require 5V power. Monitor SPI communication in the Logs tab if issues persist.
tips.fanatec: Ensure CS (chip select) pin is correctly configured. Power supply voltage is critical - verify with multimeter. Check the official pinout diagram for your specific device.
tips.custom: Start with Mode 0 and Latch UP. Check your device datasheet for correct latch and pulse timings.
tips.tm_style: If buttons float, check if pull-up resistor is present or if unused input pins are grounded.
tips.style_165: The OUT marking on boards is for cascading. The microcontroller must be connected to IN.
tips.vpc: VPC grips are noise sensitive. Use shielded cables if possible.
